slides.kicker: // sample template · food and beverage brand
slides.lead: Demo (sample) presentation template. All text is dummy and serves only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Place the event name, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight a word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so no images are needed.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Shelf presence, taste, and the biggest gaps.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Quality on the plate earns the first buy, but the story on the label earns the second. Value grows where both meet.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter opener.

items: repeat purchase rate after the recipe refresh
average taste rating across blind panels
bullets: Measure loyalty, not just trial: the second jar pays the bills.
Start where the product already wins on taste.
A small batch with a clear metric beats a big launch without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: We buy close to the grove, not the broker.
bullets: Single-estate olives picked at peak ripeness.
Cold pressing within hours of harvest.
Direct to shelf with full traceability.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same olive, two approaches and two price stories.
cards: Bottled to compete on price. Thin margin, no loyalty.
Made for a retailer's name. Volume, but no brand of our own.
Sold under our story and standard. Highest margin and recall.
Hero line as signature, the rest as steady volume.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparisons or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a food brand.
cards: Where the ingredients come from, told honestly.
A standard buyers can trust at a glance.
Short ingredient lists, nothing to hide.
One item that defines the whole range.
Customers who tell friends without being asked.
The right shelves in the right cities.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of building blocks or capabilities.

items: Growth slows, so it pays to deepen loyalty on the core line.
A hero product catches on and pulls the range with it.
Disciplined city-by-city expansion at a measured pace.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: It is not the brand with the most products that wins, but the one whose first taste makes you want a second.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Every product line carries the name of a person who answers for it.
You know in advance how you will call it a success.
On every batch, the panel has the final say.
slides.notes: A plain slide with bullets and a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the space.

slides.lead: A launch with no metric and no owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the table and run a quick panel live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
